Intraday post (23/Feb)

Weekly post:

Steep long iron fly spread | Weekly post (23-27/Feb)

Python quant course is already available on my Substack-site, by an ex-Wall Street quant.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

We have a clear net positive speed profile, customers are buying vol into both direction, but like 3x times more vol is bought to the downside. Upside vvol is sold.

Centroid is at 6840.30 - 6856.17This is sticky locally into the PM session, can force the spot to a low-probability fragile rangebound between 6835 and 6882

Upside pivot is at 6898.39target is 6938.72Stabilization can come only above the pivot. The target is very sticky, if spot can hold above 6910.However, to break above the pivot (plus I’d add 6905 too) is hard. Higher upside vol and call skew bid is needed. It would be easier if spot would open above 6890 then show strenght at the pivot during the AM.

Downside pivot is at 6830.01target is 6791.65The target is a reversion target. Spot is most likely gonna reverse, if touches it. However, the pivot from below, becomes a “momentum-braker”.

The main bet is a wider, more volatile rangebound around the centroid. Even if the market retests the lows. Upside move is more problematic, but it is the secondary bet, as market is locally betting on vol up/spot up move.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
